steps.title: Start with the given equation
steps.content: The given equation is $\frac{y - 34}{x} = p$. To express $y$ in terms of $p$ and $x$, isolate $y$.

steps.title: Isolate y algebraically
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $x$:
$$y - 34 = px$$

Add 34 to both sides:
$$y = px + 34$$

This is choice B.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A ($y = 34x + p$): Rearranging gives $\frac{y-p}{x} = 34$, not $p$. Eliminated.
  • C ($y = 34px$): Rearranging gives $\frac{y}{34x} = p$, not $\frac{y-34}{x}$. Eliminated.
  • D ($y = \frac{px}{34}$): Rearranging gives $\frac{34y}{x} = p$, not $\frac{y-34}{x}$. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
